title: Worker that explicitly handles the :snooze path under load
prompt: Write `MyApp.Workers.ThrottledAPIWorker` (queue `:external_api`, max_attempts 30). The API has a sliding-window rate limit: 100 requests per minute per account. perform/1 takes `%{"account_id" => id}`. Before calling the API, it checks `MyApp.RateLimiter.request_token(id)` which returns either `:ok` or `{:throttled, wait_seconds}`. On `:throttled`, return `{:snooze, wait_seconds}` — this puts the job back in the scheduled state for exactly wait_seconds, which is better than raising (wastes an attempt) and better than sleeping (blocks the worker slot). On `:ok`, call `MyApp.API.fetch(id)` and return `:ok` or `{:error, reason}`. Write to `lib/my_app/workers/throttled_api_worker.ex`.
tier_rationale: Combines a pre-check pattern with the snooze return — tests that the author avoids sleeping AND raising.
